content:
# session_4 — recommendations for next plan iter

## Headline
- iter-004 banked the 2 genuine corepresentability wins (`h`, `H_represents`) + 1 conjunct (`ext_finite_flat` openness). Sorry 8→6. **All remaining 6 sorries are opaque-Prop / opaque-object conclusions** — structurally unprovable as currently stubbed. The cheap proof-filling lane is now EXHAUSTED.

## Do NOT re-assign (blocked, opaque-conclusion — no constructor)
Plan agent: do not dispatch provers at these without a structural change first (they have never been attempted because no term can inhabit an opaque `Prop`):
- `H_tensor_invertible` (225), `H_tensor` (234) — `Nonempty (≅)` between opaque `H`/`tensorMod`.
- `H_locallyFree_of_ext_vanishing` (252) — opaque `IsLocallyFreeOfFiniteRankOn`.
- `exists_free_resolution_step` (263) — opaque `IsFreeMod`/`IsLFP`.
- `exists_acyclic_surjection` (242) — existence over `extGroup` of pullback; no acyclic-resolution infra.
- `ext_finite_flat` 2nd conjunct (274 inner sorry) — opaque `IsLFPAndFlatOn`.

## The one real next move: start P1b (mathlib-build)
- P1a is done. The blueprint has NOT yet decomposed the opaque→real-def construction. **Next plan iter should advance the strategy to P1b**: pick ONE opaque predicate (recommend `IsLocallyFreeOfFiniteRankOn` — it gates the headline thm 1.3 `H_locallyFree`) and have the blueprint/effort-breaker decompose its real definition into `\uses`-linked sub-lemmas, then scaffold a real `def` (not `opaque`) so the dependent theorem becomes provable. Until an opaque stub gets a real def, its dependents stay frozen.
- Recommended ordering for P1b targets (by leverage): `IsLocallyFreeOfFiniteRankOn` → unlocks `H_locallyFree_of_ext_vanishing` (1.3) via `External.halfexact_free` + `H_represents` (now proven). Then `IsLFPAndFlatOn` → `ext_finite_flat` 2nd conjunct.

## Coverage debt (planner to blueprint)
- `archon dag-query unmatched` still returns 2 `lean_aux` nodes despite the prover marking them `private`:
  - `MR0555258CompactifyingPicard.instNonemptyModules` (`private instance : Nonempty X.Modules := ⟨0⟩`)
  - `MR0555258CompactifyingPicard.instNonemptyModulesFunctor` (`private instance : Nonempty (S.Modules ⥤ X.Modules) := ⟨0⟩`)
  - **`private` did NOT drop them from the leandag scan** (the iter-003/004 plan assumption was wrong). Either (a) add a one-line blueprint `\lean{}` def-stub for each (Lean-technical `Nonempty` witnesses from the zero object), or (b) confirm with the leandag config whether `private` is meant to exclude — if so this is a scanner discrepancy to flag. Cheapest: two trivial blueprint entries to restore 1-to-1 coverage.

## Reusable pattern (verified this iter)
- **Corepresentability counit triad:** given `External.<thm> … .IsCorepresentable` `haveI`'d in scope: representing object `:= F.coreprX`; universal element `:= F.coreprx`; representability `:= ⟨F.coreprW⟩`. All defeq one-liners. `CategoryTheory.Functor.{coreprX,coreprx,coreprW}` in `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Yoneda`. Reuse for any other "representing object from an EGA existence axiom" node.

## Axioms (recurring, user action)
- 6 `External.*` axioms re-flagged by the doctor every iter. Not a defect (intentional EGA/OB boundary). TO_USER already advises the user to list them in `archon-protected.yaml` (user-only file) to silence the flag. No in-loop fix.
